Hey guys, welcome back to lesson two of module one. In this lesson we're going to dive a little bit deeper into what it means to coach the conversion. This is going to be a slightly shorter lesson than the others. But that said, it's really, really imperative before we move in deeper to really kind of ground these concepts and really steep ourselves in these insights and what they mean and how we're going to apply them later on. So while it is short, it might be a good idea to rewatch it maybe two or three times and just jot down any big insights or thoughts that come up for you. So with all that said, here are the four foundations. The first one is that every email is a sales email. In truth, there is no such thing as a welcome email, a nurture email, a value email, an educational email, or all these other types of emails we're told we could write. Sure those are kind of convenient identifiers, but at its core, every email is a sales email. And you'll see throughout the program how value and education are actually just naturally built into the process. But for now what I want you to do is drop the distinction and understand that every email is selling something. However, it's rarely, if ever, selling the thing itself. Because here's the thing, a sale does not exist in a silo. It's the result of a series of conditions, commitments, reframes, understandings, beliefs, insights, and a has that preceded. And it's our job as marketers to strategically engineer the context in which that conversion can naturally occur. So everybody knows this guy. Just how some smart dude named Einstein once said that no problem can be solved from the same level of consciousness of which it was created, I like to think that no prospect can buy from the same belief system or paradigm in which their problems or desires arose. And that's why in Foundation 3, instead of selling the thing, your product, your course, your program, whatever it is, we focus on coaching the precursors or micro conversions that need to happen before the actual sale even has a chance to take place. So if you're still with me, I do want to ground this a little bit in an example, really kind of elucidate and bring it further. If you wanted to sell me this $700 faux fur winter jacket, there are many things you'd have to coach me through first. First I mean you would have to be contextually aware enough to make sure I live in a cold place and that I was aware and intimately attuned with the perils of living in a cold place. Stuff like hypothermia and what could happen if my car stalls and I'm caught outside in minus 30 degree weather. You'd also have to convince me or coach me why I should care about ethically sourced materials and make me believe that those materials are just as effective if not more so than their cruel furrier counterparts. And you'd also have to show me probably how the price tag pays itself off since this jacket, the materials, would last a lifetime. And if you really want me to kind of pull out my credit card, you'd probably have to make me feel like I would be a better person, a more ethical person for having done so. So there's a lot at play. And this one purchase, a lot of micro conversions, a lot of precursors that you need to coach me through before generating that final sale. And if you want to take a more immediate or recognizable example, we could take a look at this course itself. Before we could even talk about 10X launches, you probably had to be coached first on the fact that, yes, copywriting is the biggest bottleneck between you and executing those big product launches and evergreen funnels. And you'd also have to be convinced or coached that it's not as scary or esoteric as you might think. In fact, the process may actually be enjoyable and very much in line with your current skills as a coach or educator. You would also need to know that you need to learn how to do this yourself. It's not just something that can be easily handed off or outsourced. Yes, there is a possibility of outsourcing it, but it's not easy. In fact, there might be even more friction in handing it off and creating the budget to hand it off to a great copywriter instead of doing it yourself. And finally, you would also have to know that this is a skill and a program that can produce immediate returns. It's not something that you need to be plugging away at for months and months and months and even maybe a year before seeing any kind of result. To get excited enough to say, yes, I want to get inside 10X launches, you have to be coached into the fact that the returns can be immediate. It can come as quickly as plugging in and writing your first theme plate, for example. So those are many of the things that we took into account in creating and marketing this program. And we're going to show you over the course of the next few modules how to take that into consideration when you're marketing your own things. So just to kind of wrap up that point, we are rarely selling the thing. Instead, we are coaching the beliefs, the reframes, the attitudes, the shifts, the understandings that empower someone to become a customer, that make it a natural fit into their new reality. And that is why marketing done well is nothing but the bridge that allows your prospect to confidently go from point A to point buyer. We're recognizing the fundamental fact that who your customer is now and who they will have to become in order to hit by our two very different people. They are a living, growing, ever-evolving avatar. Certain beliefs need to be instilled, certain past mistakes forgiven and redeemed, and certain fears and resistance overcome. And we're going to teach you the order of these microconversions, how they need to take place across your entire launch funnel. We're going to take a look at that deeper in module two when we look at the buyer's journey in more detail. But for now, here's the enduring point. I want you to write down and tattoo to your brain's gray matter. Even pin it to your Bolton board so you just remember it at all times when engineering these funnels and when writing your copy. It's this. And as a coach, educator, and change maker, why would you wait until after someone buys to start coaching, educating, or catalyzing change? The opportunity is in front of you every time someone opts into your funnel. The transformation doesn't only occur once they buy your product and get inside your membership area. The transformation begins the moment they opt in, raise their hand and say, yes, I am ready to change my life. So don't wait until they're inside your members area to care deeply. Instead, I really invite you to recognize that the person who opted in and the person putting down the credit card are two very different people with a different set of beliefs and understandings. And it's your job as the conversion coach to map out the transformation that has to occur and weave it strategically through your sales messaging. So in module two, as I said, we'll take a deeper look at exactly where those mini transformations or precursors take place within your funnel. And then in modules three to seven, you'll actually get the sequences and the theme place that we use to activate them and bring them to life. But before we get there, just a few more quick lessons to really drive home the overarching process. So give this video a quick rewatch if you have to. And when you're confident, when you feel really grounded and steeped in these principles, meet me in the next lesson where we'll, you know, drive over a few more notes on the art of sequencing email campaigns. So I will see you in the next lesson.
